behaviors.description: Cache key generation produces identical keys for identical query parameters and different keys for different parameters, with no collisions.
behaviors.justification: Pure deterministic function with no external dependencies, timing, or I/O. Identical inputs must always produce identical outputs; different inputs must produce different outputs. This is local logic that can be fully tested with in-process function calls. A real failure (determinism bug, collision) would be caught by a Unit test.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test that calls the key generation function with multiple sets of identical parameters (verifying deterministic output), with multiple sets of distinct parameters (verifying no collisions), and with edge cases (empty parameters, special characters, large parameter sets, parameter reordering). Assert keys are equal/different as expected.

behaviors.description: Cache entry TTL is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles=5 minutes, product catalog=1 hour).
behaviors.justification: While the TTL calculation formula could be Unit tested with mocked rules, the behavior depends on the correct rule being loaded for each data category. A Unit test with mocked rules would miss bugs in category-to-rule mapping, configuration parsing, or rule loading. Real failures (wrong rule for category, rule not found, configuration not loaded) require testing with actual configuration and category-rule associations loaded from config.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that loads configuration with defined freshness rules for multiple data categories (e.g., user profiles=5 min, product catalog=1 hour, real-time data=10 sec). Create cache entries for each category and verify each gets the correct TTL value based on its category. Test that non-existent categories fail appropriately and that rule changes in configuration are reflected in TTL computation.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds configured threshold, the eviction strategy automatically switches from LRU to LFU as configured.
behaviors.justification: While threshold-comparison logic could be Unit tested with mocked memory readings, this would miss bugs in how the cache actually measures and tracks its own memory usage. Real failures (incorrect memory accounting, off-by-one errors in tracking, missing some data structures) only surface when real cache entries are inserted and memory grows. Integration level is needed to populate the cache with real data and verify that the memory measurement correctly detects when usage exceeds the threshold.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that loads configuration with a memory threshold (e.g., 1 GB or 100 MB for testing). Populate the cache with entries of known size until total memory usage exceeds the threshold. Verify that the eviction strategy automatically switches from LRU to LFU. Monitor memory during insertions to confirm threshold detection is accurate. Test multiple threshold values and cache entry sizes to ensure the comparison logic works correctly.

behaviors.description: Cache entries are invalidated when the underlying PostgreSQL table is written to, with only entries matching affected rows being removed.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on actual PostgreSQL transaction behavior, trigger firing, or write-listener detection. Mocking the database write would miss real failures in trigger logic, transaction isolation, or how the application detects writes. Real failures (triggers not fired, stale entries not invalidated, wrong rows identified, incorrect key matching) require testing with a real (test) PostgreSQL instance to verify that actual table writes trigger cache invalidation with correct row-to-cache-key mapping.
behaviors.test_description: System test with test PostgreSQL instance. Insert cache entries with keys derived from specific row identifiers (e.g., user_id=123, user_id=456). Perform write operations (UPDATE, DELETE) on the underlying table affecting specific rows. Verify that cache entries whose keys match the affected row IDs are invalidated. Verify that non-matching keys remain in cache. Test both single-row and multi-row write operations, and verify that the invalidation is transactionally correct (e.g., invalidation occurs after the write commits).

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: The eviction logic (tracking recency and frequency, identifying the least-used entry, removal) is deterministic and can be fully tested with simulated usage patterns in-process. Use mocked clocks to control timing and manual counters to control access frequency. No real I/O, external timing, or subprocess dependencies are needed. Real failures (wrong entry identified, off-by-one in LRU queue, frequency counter bugs) are caught by Unit tests with exhaustive usage simulation.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test that creates a cache with a small capacity limit (e.g., 5 entries). Populate it with multiple entries. Simulate usage patterns using mocked clock: access entries at different times to establish LRU order, then with different frequencies to establish LFU order. Mark cache as full and trigger eviction. Under LRU policy, verify that the least-recently-accessed entry is removed. Switch to LFU policy and repeat, verifying that the least-frequently-accessed entry is removed. Test edge cases (ties in recency/frequency, single-entry cache, single-access patterns).
